user_query:
I’m considering leading a seed round in a logistics SaaS startup called ParcelLoop. Please do a full investment evaluation and due diligence using the materials provided. Read all files under input/ and produce the following outputs under output/:

1) Write a comprehensive investment memo to output/investment_memo.md. Include clearly labeled sections: Team, Market (with TAM and competitive landscape), Product, Why Now, Defensibility, Unit Economics (compute CAC:LTV with stated assumptions), Burn Rate, Runway (in months), Revenue Quality (recurring vs one-time; concentration), Gross Margin, Term Sheet (summary of your proposed terms), Portfolio Strategy (position sizing and reserve plan), Exit Considerations, Red Flags, Diligence Plan, and Decision (go/no-go with rationale).

2) Create a structured risk register at output/risk_register.csv with the header: risk_id,category,description,severity,likelihood,mitigation,owner. Include at least 6 distinct risks spanning Market, Financial, and Governance categories (others optional).

3) Draft a proposed term sheet in output/term_sheet.yaml containing keys: valuation_pre_money, round_size, security_type, liquidation_preference, anti_dilution, pro_rata_rights, board_composition, protective_provisions, option_pool_increase, closing_conditions. Use clear, non-empty values for each key.

4) Analyze the current cap table and expected post-round ownership. Write output/cap_table_analysis.csv with header: stakeholder,shares,share_class,ownership_post_percent,notes. Include founders, the new round, and option pool entries at minimum.

5) List specific red flags you identified (from both qualitative and quantitative signals) in output/red_flags.json as a JSON array of strings (at least 3 items).

Where needed, make reasonable, explicitly stated assumptions and keep them conservative. Do not fabricate customer quotes or financial figures beyond what’s in input/. Reference which file informed each major conclusion.

Files to read in input/: startup_overview.md, financials.csv, customers.jsonl, cap_table.json, metrics.tsv.
